title.rendered: Day 22 of $QQQ short term up-trend; GMI=GREEN

content.rendered: The 10 week average is back above its 30 week average. GMI = 6.

GMI is red. I am in cash. Patience.

Here is a long discussion of why I use the 30 week moving average and how the General Market Index works across six components and what each one means and why I wait for confirmation before committing capital to the market in size, etc etc, padding this body past the long-form word threshold so the heuristic classifies it as long-form rather than a daily update or unknown. More words. And more. Several more sentences here to be safe about the count. Filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler filler. The thirty week moving average is a key tool for trend identification in weekly charts of major indices. When price is above the thirty week average the market is in a long term up trend and I lean bullish in my positioning. When price crosses below I move to cash or short exposure. The six components of the General Market Index each measure a different aspect of market health including breadth momentum and leadership. Breadth measures how many stocks are participating in the advance. Momentum measures the rate of change. Leadership measures whether growth and technology stocks are outperforming defensive and value sectors. All six must align before I add risk. Patience is the most important discipline in investing. Waiting for confirmation prevents premature entries and reduces drawdowns significantly over time. The thirty week average has been reliable across multiple market cycles since I started tracking it in the early two thousands. Additional filler words to ensure we surpass the six hundred word threshold for the long form classification test case in this fixture file.
